title: Exponential Growth, Decay, and Logarithmic Scales · Mastery Check
summary: Model adoption, cooling, depreciation, and sound-level measurements. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an exponential and logarithmic scenario lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish additive change from multiplicative growth and derive period factors.
Fit exponential growth and decay parameters with explicit initial conditions.
Use logarithms as inverses to solve for time, rate, or exponent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: $1100 grows once per year at 4.5% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1100, r=0.045, and t=2.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use P(1 + r)^t with P=1100, r=0.045, and t=2.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.01 of the exact value and includes $ or dollars.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Growth Limitations applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify saturation, regime changes, and extrapolation risks in exponential models.
